mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits immediately to equity valuations by lowering discount rates and easing financial conditions, which pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days when the policy path is still downward.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of months with a declining FEDFUNDS trend, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth in RSAFS crosses above its recent baseline, it signals stronger final demand and rising near-term revenue expectations, which lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show higher 20-day forward returns than untriggered observations, or if positive-return hit rate under the trigger is below 55% over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend marks labor-market deterioration and weaker household income expectations, which compresses earnings forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of labor-deterioration triggers, or if the signal's rolling 2-year IC is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A broad slowdown in INDPRO signals weaker industrial activity and tighter realized corporate revenue momentum, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days when industrial production is contracting.
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO growth does not coincide with below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears and rolling Rank IC exceeds +0.03 for any 2-year span.

mechanism_narrative: Joint weakness in GDPC1 and PAYEMS identifies a real-demand recession signal; when output and payroll growth both stall, earnings revisions worsen and S&P 500 returns are negative over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined GDP-plus-payroll weakness regime does not produce negative 60-day forward returns more often than not, or if either proxy alone explains the effect as well as the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL creates a tightening-real-rate regime that hurts multiples and risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the high-rate/high-inflation trigger does not correspond to negative 5-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not stronger than each single series effect.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS is rising while UNRATE is falling, the labor market is improving and household income expectations strengthen, supporting risk assets and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the payroll-expansion and unemployment-decline combination fails to outperform baseline 60-day returns, or if the signal becomes negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-rate-cut stabilization phase, rising HOUST indicates lower financing stress and improving housing demand, which leads S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days through the wealth and cyclicals channels.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months after FEDFUNDS has stopped increasing
failure_condition: Reject if housing starts improvement after policy stabilization does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS is not flat-to-lower.
